title: No More Battles for Paul Thomas Anderson
text: Paul Thomas Anderson finally achieved Oscar recognition after decades of nominations without wins. At the 98th Academy Awards, he won three statuettes for his film "One Battle After Another": Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Producer. Anderson's victory ended a historic losing streak. He had previously been nominated across six Academy Award ceremonies without winning, with acclaimed films like "Boogie Nights" and "There Will Be Blood" losing to other contenders. "One Battle After Another" dominated the awards season, winning major precursor awards from the BAFTAs, Golden Globes, and Producers Guild. The film, which reunites Anderson with Leonardo DiCaprio, became his highest-grossing film and was widely called one of 2025's best movies. The Best Picture race proved exceptionally competitive between "One Battle After Another" and Ryan Coogler's vampire thriller "Sinners." A late SAG Award win by "Sinners'" Michael B. Jordan briefly suggested a potential upset, but Anderson's film ultimately prevailed.
